Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–585, § 2(c)90 Stat. 2902Oct. 21, 1976“The provisions of this section [enacting this section and provisions set out as a note under ] shall be effective with respect to benefits payable for months after June 1977.”section 1382e of this title

children.text: after the calendar quarter in which it first makes such supplementary payments,
continue to make such supplementary payments, and

maintain such supplementary payments at levels which are not lower than the levels of such payments in effect in December 1976, or, if no such payments were made in that month, the levels for the first subsequent month in which such payments were made.

The Commissioner of Social Security shall not find that a State has failed to meet the requirements imposed by paragraph (4) of subsection (a) with respect to the levels of its supplementary payments for a particular month or months if the State’s expenditures for such payments in the twelve-month period (within which such month or months fall) beginning on the effective date of any increase in the level of supplemental security income benefits pursuant to are not less than its expenditures for such payments in the preceding twelve-month period.section 1382f of this title

For purposes of determining under paragraph (1) whether a State’s expenditures for supplementary payments in the 12-month period beginning on the effective date of any increase in the level of supplemental security income benefits are not less than the State’s expenditures for such payments in the preceding 12-month period, the Commissioner of Social Security, in computing the State’s expenditures, shall disregard, pursuant to a 1-time election of the State, all expenditures by the State for retroactive supplementary payments that are required to be made in connection with the retroactive supplemental security income benefits referred to in section 5041 of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1990.

children.heading: Election to apply subsection (a)(4)
children.label: c Election to apply subsection (a)(4)
children.text: Any State which satisfies the requirements of this section solely by reason of subsection (b) for a particular month or months in any 12-month period (described in such subsection) ending on or after , may elect, with respect to any month in any subsequent 12-month period (so described), to apply subsection (a)(4) as though the reference to December 1976 in such subsection were a reference to the month of December which occurred in the 12-month period immediately preceding such subsequent period.June 30, 1982

children.heading: Determinations respecting any portion of period , throughJuly 1, 1980June 30, 1981
children.label: d Determinations respecting any portion of period , throughJuly 1, 1980June 30, 1981
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall not find that a State has failed to meet the requirements imposed by paragraph (4) of subsection (a) with respect to the levels of its supplementary payments for any portion of the period , through , if the State’s expenditures for such payments in that twelve-month period were not less than its expenditures for such payments for the period , through (or, if the State made no supplementary payments in the period , through , the expenditures for the first twelve-month period extending from July 1 through June 30 in which the State made such payments).July 1, 1980June 30, 1981July 1, 1976June 30, 1977July 1, 1976June 30, 1977

children.heading: Meeting subsection (a)(4) requirements for any month after March 1983
children.label: e Meeting subsection (a)(4) requirements for any month after March 1983
A
children.text: the combined level of its supplementary payments (to recipients of the type involved) and the amounts payable (to or on behalf of such recipients) under and , for that particular month,section 1382(b) of this titlesection 211(a)(1)(A) of Public Law 93–66

children.label: B
children.text: the combined level of its supplementary payments (to recipients of the type involved) and the amounts payable (to or on behalf of such recipients) under and , for March 1983, increased by the amount of all cost-of-living adjustments under (and any other benefit increases under this subchapter) which have occurred after March 1983 and before that particular month.section 1382(b) of this titlesection 211(a)(1)(A) of Public Law 93–66section 1382f of this title

In determining the amount of any increase in the combined level involved under paragraph (1)(B) of this subsection, any portion of such amount which would otherwise be attributable to the increase under shall be deemed instead to be equal to the amount of the cost-of-living adjustment which would have occurred in July 1983 (without regard to the 3-percent limitation contained in ) if section 111 of the Social Security Amendments of 1983 had not been enacted.section 1382f(c) of this titlesection 415(i)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Passthrough relating to optional State supplementation
children.label: f Passthrough relating to optional State supplementation
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall not find that a State has failed to meet the requirements imposed by subsection (a) with respect to the levels of its supplementary payments for the period , through , if in the period , through , its supplementary payment levels (other than to recipients of benefits determined under ) are not less than those in effect in December 1976, increased by a percentage equal to the percentage by which payments under and have been increased as a result of all adjustments under section 1382f(a) and (c) of this title which have occurred after December 1976 and before February 1986.January 1, 1984December 31, 1985January 1, 1986December 31, 1986section 1382(e)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1382(b) of this titlesection 211(a)(1)(A) of Public Law 93–66

children.label: g Mandatory pass-through of increased personal needs allowance
